summary: Wrap SELECT/WITH queries in `SELECT * FROM (<query>) LIMIT N` when --limit is appended so trailing line/block comments can't comment out the LIMIT; extend stripSQLStringLiterals to also skip `"…"` identifiers, `--` line comments, and `/* … */` block comments before the mutation-keyword check.
reason: Greptile P2 review findings on PR #676 (sixth pass summary, 5/5 confidence): (1) `--limit N` appended via Sprintf was silently swallowed when the user's query ended with a trailing comment, yielding unbounded rows with no error; subquery-wrap makes the LIMIT non-bypassable. (2) The mutation-keyword regex still false-positived on `SELECT "create" FROM messages` (double-quoted identifier) and `SELECT /* update later */ id FROM messages` (block comment) because stripSQLStringLiterals only handled single-quoted strings. Both are now stripped before the keyword scan. Driver-level mode=ro remains the real write barrier; these are usability fixes to the defence-in-depth layer.
